description: A build method starts requests, mutates state, or creates controllers, so they fire repeatedly as the framework rebuilds.
body: Failure mode: build() contains a network call, a setState, or controller/subscription creation, but the framework may call build many times per frame and per rebuild, so the side effect fires repeatedly, duplicating requests and leaking newly-created controllers. Detection signals: duplicate network calls; controllers created inside build; setState triggered from build causing rebuild loops; counts that grow with rebuilds. Do not perform side effects or allocate resources in build. Safer fallback: keep build a pure function of state, move side effects to initState/event handlers, create controllers once in initState, and verify build is idempotent with a widget test.
